ops.title: 2026-07-19 guard-fix session — local parser/pack verification lies (v8 skew, cached-pack summoning), the isolation recipe, and shared-@ ship-around-foreign-bookmark mechanics

ops.body:
Shipped 2026-07-19/20 on main: grep guard bounded-existence fix (0c0b1587), web guard assignment-captured sink lane (022bf455), released-parser pin fix-forward (abe83002). Pack bump COMPLETE 2026-07-20: after the concurrent session's 0.9.0 + `[tools.*]` toml work landed (6000b25a, its release commit left un-tagged), completed their orphaned release by tagging v0.28.3 at their commit, then b37cf076 bumped pack→0.10.0 + plugin.json→0.28.4 and tagged v0.28.4 (both releases green). The plugin-cache updater tracks main's plugin.json at USER scope automatically (autoUpdate pulled 0.28.4 within minutes of the push); project-scope pins refresh only at session start in that project — don't chase them, and `claude plugin update` from another cwd still operates at user scope.

**Verification traps that cost a red CI run** (also in the repo papercut journal):
- `uv run --project ../captain-hook` = UNRELEASED cc-transcript v8 parser (path override). v8 folds string-embedded substitutions (`echo "$(curl …)"`); the RELEASED parser surfaces them span-less. Parser probes made through the sibling checkout lie about CI. CI mirror: `uv run --no-project --with capt-hook`.
- `capt-hook --hooks <dir> run` silently loads the registered plugin-cache pack alongside the tree (via `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR` + real-HOME config; the dir's own `[pack] name="ccx"` manifest summons the cached pack by name). Full isolation: `env -u CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR -u CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR HOME=<scratch>` + a manifest-free copy of the hooks dir. Inline `test` rows are per-pack self-contained and don't have this problem.

**Ship-around-foreign-bookmark recipe (worked cleanly):** shared `@` parked on another session's non-trunk lineage → `ccx vcs ship` refuses (correct, mutates nothing). Manual lane: `jj commit <paths>` ×2 (scoped; foreign edits stay in `@`), `jj rebase -r <c1> -d main` then `-r <c2> -d <c1>` (children auto-reparent back; foreign stack untouched), `jj bookmark move main --to <c2>`, `jj git push --bookmark main`. AFTER the rebase the working copy shows the FOREIGN lineage's file versions — any "verify" run against the tree is then vacuous for your change; use `jj workspace add <dir> -r main` for fix-forwards (jj works there; git/ccx-vcs don't — push from the primary checkout; `jj workspace forget` when done).

**Bash-tool script traps:** `for id in $ids` does NOT word-split newline lists here (fish semantics) — two CI watch loops died passing `id1\nid2\nid3` as one URL; watch runs by literal id instead. The ccx-format PreToolUse wrapper re-encodes `gh … --json … -q` output into a JSON array literal unless the output is piped (`| sort`, `| cat`).

Related: [[mcp-json-fleet-rollout]] (codex edits, Claude ships), [[capt-hook-pack-test-cache-and-mode]] (registered-pack cache), [[jj-shared-workingcopy-collision]].
